compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
ARCHITECTURE DECISION (brainstorm outcome, grounded across 3 deep-research rounds + codebase/web spikes; full trail in session log ad3f-2d99-6d41-40e6 'boom-demon-jug'):

CONVERGED STACK (pressure-tested; evidence SUPPORTS it):
1. ENGINE = a thin homegrown synchronous declarative INTERPRETER over a tailored lean YAML/JSON IR (steps + conditionals + loops + map/fan-out), with run-state on rebar's event log. Building (not adopting) is EVIDENCE-BACKED: every durable-execution library (DBOS=Postgres/SQLite, Restate=server+own log) OWNS its persistence and none can defer to an external git-backed event log; the off-the-shelf wheels don't fit the event-sourced axle. SpiffWorkflow/BPMN rejected as the engine (owns run-state via whole-workflow serialization; heavy BPMN data model; no native agent step; LGPL).
2. AGENT RUNTIME = a PROVIDER-AGNOSTIC runtime behind the existing Runner seam, REPLACING LangChain/LangGraph (its sole original justification, native Langfuse tracing, is gone — Langfuse is OTel-native in 2026). HARD REQUIREMENT (user): cross-provider; an Anthropic-only solution is REJECTED -> the raw-anthropic-SDK+output_format option is OUT. Leading: Pydantic AI (pydantic-ai-slim[anthropic,...]) with structured output via PromptedOutput (provider-agnostic AND avoids the Claude thinking + forced-tool-choice 400). Keep LangGraph only as the per-agent-step runtime until the swap is proven. ENFORCE the seam strictly (Pydantic AI has 2.0-beta churn + a maintainer-bandwidth crunch) so the dep stays swappable.
3. VISUAL EDITING = a thin IR<->standard-format serializer feeding an OFF-THE-SHELF embeddable editor (bpmn-js or draw.io) as an EPHEMERAL projection, NEVER persisted. Validated by n8n/Windmill cautionary tales (DB-vs-file dual-store reconciliation pain) and Kestra's compact declarative agent-step encoding (the template to copy). Visual format choice (bpmn-js vs draw.io) settled by a round-trip POC.

CONTROL FLOW (executor spike): conditionals/loops trip the documented Burr triggers; resolved by a synchronous worklist interpreter (still no async/threading -> tripwire intact, override recorded). Iteration-keyed run-state markers + mandatory max_iterations (each iteration is a committed, auto-pushed event).

DE-RISK POCs (next, grounded in the Inspect AI eval seam + 3 corpuses): (1) RUNTIME — Pydantic AI behind the seam, PromptedOutput, on >=2 PROVIDERS, asserting structured-output validity + zero-400 under extended thinking + Langfuse-OTel trace fidelity; (2) ENGINE — thin interpreter, prove resume/replay correctness over the event log (kill mid-run -> replay from events -> no double-effects); (3) VISUAL — IR<->editor round-trip property test (bpmn-js vs draw.io), semantic + ID + agent-metadata fidelity, visual never persisted. Experiments live under docs/experiments (reference, not deliverable).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCOPE SPLIT (per brainstorm, session log ad3f): the workflow-engine BUILD is broken out into its OWN epic e6d9-41da-da0d-44ef (hump-seam-spice) and removed from this remediation epic's scope. e6d9 owns: declarative IR + thin interpreter (run-state on the event log = genuine reuse; bounded-concurrent fan-out) + provider-agnostic Pydantic AI runtime (replacing LangGraph) + ephemeral bpmn-js visual editing + the structured-output reliability stack that RETIRES the second-interpreter LLM (native/strict decoding + json-repair + Pydantic validators + bounded same-model retry). This epic (6f2d) now depends_on e6d9 and RETAINS the remediation-on-top scope that runs ON the new engine: WS-DOC (docs/workflow-engine.md), WS-MIGRATE (migrate verify_completion + existing reviewers onto e6d9; build the plan-review gate on it), WS-PROMPTS, WS-EVAL-EXISTING, WS-CLEANUP, WS-VISUAL-EVAL, WS-INTENT-ALIGN. WS-VALIDATE is RESOLVED: keep-and-rebuild (no off-the-shelf engine fits the event-sourced + git-text + mixed-LLM constraints; DBOS/Restate/Spiff all own their run-state). The engine-build prose still in this description is retained for history but is now OWNED by e6d9.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
RE-TRIAGE (post-5fd2 + post-e6d9 close): several retained workstreams are already SATISFIED by the now-closed gate epic 5fd2 and engine epic e6d9; the epic prose predates their close. Per-WS status with evidence:

DONE:
- WS-INTENT-ALIGN — (a) the intent-source-fidelity (ISF) criterion was BUILT + VALIDATED under closed story 681b (fire-acid-peer, parent 5fd2): wired in registry.py (ISF, facet scope-intent), prompt files src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_ISF.md + isf_finder.md + isf_summarizer.md, eval src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml. (b) built-vs-intent visual-editing reconciliation was done in the brainstorm (log ad3f boom-demon-jug) → the silently-deferred bidirectional visual editing was RESTORED as ephemeral bpmn-js inside e6d9 (closed). Nothing left here.

SUBSTANTIALLY DONE:
- WS-PROMPTS — the gate's PRODUCTION prompts already live in the git-native prompt library src/rebar/llm/reviewers/*.md (44 contract-bearing files incl. completion_verifier.md, code_quality.md, plan_review_* incl. coach/finder/container/ISF), loaded via get_prompt + .rebar/prompts/ overrides + index.json drift gate (registry.py calls this 'the workflow-engine prompt library'). The docs/experiments criteria_v*.json are NOT in use (the experiment-as-deliverable trap was avoided). RESIDUAL: confirm the prompts live on the NEW e6d9 engine once the gate/verifier are migrated — a sliver coupled to WS-MIGRATE.

OPEN (genuinely remaining):
- WS-DOC — docs/workflow-engine.md does NOT exist.
- WS-MIGRATE — the plan-review gate is still a BESPOKE orchestrator (orchestrator.py fans out via ThreadPoolExecutor, not the e6d9 interpreter); verify_completion is a direct agent call (completion.py, no engine). Neither runs on the new engine.
- WS-EVAL-EXISTING — PARTIAL: eval_specs cover plan-review (finder/verifier/isf) + code-quality; verify_completion / review_ticket / scan_spec have NO labeled eval set yet.
- WS-CLEANUP — examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py scaffold still present; removal set pending Joe's selection.
- WS-VISUAL-EVAL — no decision artifact found (e6d9 delivered workflow visual editing, but the prompt+eval-STATUS review-surface decision — Langfuse/Inspect/promptfoo first-class-or-out — appears unrecorded).

NARROWED REMAINING SCOPE for 6f2d: WS-DOC, WS-MIGRATE, WS-EVAL-EXISTING, WS-CLEANUP, WS-VISUAL-EVAL (+ the WS-PROMPTS-on-new-engine sliver). DECISION (this session): WS-CLEANUP + WS-MIGRATE + WS-PROMPTS-completion are being split into a NEW dedicated epic (to be brainstormed); 6f2d retains WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING + WS-VISUAL-EVAL. ACs for WS-INTENT-ALIGN, the visual-editing decision, and prompts-in-library are effectively met (via 681b/5fd2 + e6d9).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay fires: this is a MIGRATION].

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 — already satisfied):
- Prior art is well-considered for the engine substrate: the dependency epic A (sage-votary-logic, 5766) ran an explicit cross-engine survey (Argo / Step Functions / dbt / LangGraph / Airflow / Dagster / Temporal), and the batch-runner design note (docs/design/batch-runner-seam.md) grounds the "engine-opaque sealed batch" choice in the industry mainline (claim-check + decide-once-journal-the-result; Temporal/SFN/Airflow/Argo). T1(b) novelty-justification is met for the batch construct.
- Strong anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three discipline: the plan reuses EXISTING engine step kinds (`if:`, `uses`, `prompt`, `branch`, `batch`) and states the completion-verifier "fits EXISTING concepts (no new constructs)"; the production batch-runner is a THIN adapter over the extracted shared sizing.py units (no duplicated algorithm). T1(a)/(b) met for the migration mechanics.
- Capability assertions are grounded, not hand-waved: the planned-trace capture seams (PlannedTraceRunner / TracingFakeRunner, FakeRunner) already exist in src/rebar/llm/workflow/trace.py, so the "offline, no-live-calls, CI-able parity" assertion is verified against present code rather than aspirational. T1(c) met for that claim.

FINDINGS (T1):

- [ ] The plan introduces "planned-trace parity (NOT result parity)" as the migration's validation pattern but does not consider the directly-relevant in-repo prior art: src/rebar/llm/parity.py already establishes a DIFFERENT parity philosophy for a prior LLM-pipeline migration (LangGraph -> Pydantic AI, ticket 7d58) — non-inferiority on the eval corpus (validity floor, >=95% verdict agreement, ZERO decision flips, recall/false-accept within +/-2pp). T1(a)/(b): justify why trace-parity is the right (and sufficient) bar here rather than the established result/non-inferiority bar — and whether the two are complementary (trace-parity proves the engine wiring issues the same planned calls; it does NOT prove the migrated gate still BLOCKS what it should). Note the plan's own seam doc concedes the verify/coach model is "NOT part of the pinned planned-trace guarantee," so trace-parity alone leaves the actual block/advisory/dropped decision unguarded across the cutover — exactly what parity.py was built to certify.
  - criteria: [T1]
  - location: Plan "What" -> "Planned-trace parity validation" bullet; AC line 3 ("A PLANNED-TRACE parity harness asserts..."); prior art at src/rebar/llm/parity.py
  - evidence: Plan asserts parity "NOT result parity (the review is LLM-dependent and variable)"; existing src/rebar/llm/parity.py module docstring: "Parity is NON-INFERIORITY... per-criterion verdict AGREEMENT >= 95% AND ZERO decision-level flips (block/advisory/dropped)"; tests/unit/test_plan_review_parity.py header concedes the non-laddered verify/coach model is "NOT part of the pinned planned-trace guarantee" — i.e. the coarse decision is not asserted by trace-parity. ABSENCE: the plan text nowhere references the existing parity.py bar or distinguishes the two approaches.
  - suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the "Planned-trace parity validation" bullet (and a clause to AC3) acknowledging src/rebar/llm/parity.py's existing non-inferiority bar and stating the relationship — e.g. "trace-parity guards the engine's planned-call WIRING offline/in-CI; it complements (does not replace) parity.py's result non-inferiority bar, which remains the guard on decision-level (block/advisory/dropped) regressions across the cutover" — or, if trace-parity is deemed sufficient, state explicitly WHY the result-decision regression risk is covered elsewhere (e.g. AC4's "signed verdicts/attestations intact" + a one-time gold-set check) so the cutover in AC4 is not authorized on wiring-equivalence alone.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T3 — Integration feasibility [overlay]): PASS, no findings.

T3 is an overlay that fires only for EXTERNAL APIs/CLIs/services/libraries or newly-asserted capabilities. This plan's "integrations" are all INTERNAL, already-built engine seams — so per the T3 ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable") the overlay is largely not-applicable. I nonetheless tool-verified each named construct the plan depends on, and every one exists and is operational:

- `batch` step + `BatchRunner` seam + reference `DefaultBatchRunner` — shipped + signed-PASS under epic A (5766, closed): `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py`, `executor.py`.
- `if:`-overlay precompute pattern (no new IR field) — verified in 5766's `interpreter.py _guard_scoped` + `steps.py` overlay_triggers.
- Production batch-runner extracted behind the seam — `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` + `sizing.py` already present.
- Planned-trace parity harness mechanism — `src/rebar/llm/workflow/trace.py` (`PlannedTraceRunner`, FakeRunner/FakeAgentRunner seam); the plan's load-bearing claim ("capture the INTENDED model, fake echoes model=None") matches trace.py L30-31/L45 exactly. A `tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_parity.py` and a `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` already exist.
- `execution_mode` call-mode (agent|1-shot) capture — grounded across prompts.py, runner.py, ADR 0001, and trace.py L207-216.
- Bespoke implementations to migrate — `plan_review/orchestrator.py` and `completion.py` both present.

No external API/CLI subcommand or endpoint to MATCH against --help/docs; no auth/HTTPS precondition is in play; no critical capability gap routing to a SPIKE. The feasibility is concretely demonstrated by already-closed epic A, so this is a PASS rather than an asserted-but-unverified-capability MAJOR.

Affirmations (T3 checklist):
- Achievable-as-described: YES — every integration construct exists and is closed/operational.
- Named subcommands/endpoints exist: N/A (internal seams), and the internal seams named all resolve in code.
- Auth/HTTPS preconditions: N/A (no external service).
- Critical-gap→SPIKE: N/A (no capability gap found).

(Non-T3 note for the author, out of this pass's scope: AC5 names `examples/code_review.yaml` + a `templates.py` scaffold as removal targets, but neither file currently exists — `examples/` holds only `review_skeleton.yaml`, and search finds no `templates.py`. The epic's own RE-TRIAGE comment flags the removal set as "pending Joe's selection." Worth reconciling, but that is a G-series/staleness concern, not a T3 integration-feasibility finding.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · G6 — Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection]

VERDICT: PASS (no findings). Reviewing the "Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine" plan against G6, grounded in the live code.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies):

1. MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — sound and complete. The plan's load-bearing mechanism is PLANNED-TRACE parity (control-flow shape) rather than result parity, correctly recognizing LLM output is non-deterministic and byte-comparison is incoherent. Grounded check confirms the mechanism works: the harness compares DET coverage on every scenario, finder traces (role + sorted criteria + call-mode + intended entry model, concurrency-order-normalized), and cross-asserts the verify call-mode (tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_parity.py; capture via src/rebar/llm/workflow/trace.py). Edge/empty/failure cases are handled: P1/P5 DET-block and P8-too-big/bug-exempt scenarios are explicitly partitioned in the corpus (tests/unit/workflow/_parity_corpus.py); the cutover (gate_dispatch.py) preserves INDETERMINATE-on-outage for plan-review and fail-closed-on-outage for completion, with signing wrappers untouched so signed verdicts stay byte-compatible (AC4).

2. The PRE-ESCALATION tolerance is a deliberate, defensible gap — not a hidden assumption. Size-ladder escalation is runtime-token-dependent and cannot be reproduced offline; the plan states this tolerance explicitly and still pins the laddered-finder ENTRY model + the budget/shed coverage, which is where the routing/chunking/ladder complexity (the real safety surface) lives. No TOCTOU / ordering / atomicity defect found.

3. FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — solves the named problem (one engine substrate, validated parity, samples removed), not a proxy. The "model=None echo" subtlety is correctly handled (capture the INTENDED/selected req.config.model, not the fake's echoed field) — trace.py confirms.

4. APPROACH SELECTION — chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good as structurally-different alternatives I generated (result-parity/golden snapshots — rejected, incoherent for LLM gates; live shadow-traffic flag-flip — slower, costs tokens, not CI-able, can't exhaustively hit P1/P5/P8/exempt/overlay edges; incremental walking-skeleton-first — the plan in fact does this by migrating completion first). The offline-corpus design wins on testability, cost, CI-ability, and edge coverage. Discarding the alternatives (not writing them into the plan).

5. POSITIVE RATIONALE present throughout (G6.4 satisfied): planned-trace because the review is LLM-variable; offline FakeRunner so edge cases are exhaustive/cheap/CI-able; extract sizing.py behind epic A's seam to avoid algorithm duplication; completion fits existing constructs (no new kinds needed). No rejected-alternatives section required (anti-priming respected).

No coaching findings for G6.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 · G6 (approach soundness) — PASS, no findings. Mechanism is sound and grounded: planned-trace (not result) parity is the correct choice for LLM-variable output; the FakeRunner/intended-model capture, pre-escalation tolerance, flag-gated cutover (verify.gate_engine), and reuse of shared sizing/pass1 units behind epic A's batch-runner seam all match the real engine implementation (workflow/trace.py, gate_dispatch.py, production_batch_runner.py, gates/*.yaml). Considered alternatives (result-parity; big-bang cutover) — both inferior on robustness/blast-radius and the plan defensibly avoids them. Positive rationale is present throughout.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
SCOPE DECISION (this session) — completing the epic's remaining workstreams.

Re-triage (confirmed against code this session): WS-INTENT-ALIGN, WS-MIGRATE, WS-PROMPTS, WS-CLEANUP, WS-VISUAL, WS-VALIDATE are all DONE via the closed sibling epics A (sage-votary-logic/5766) + B (peak-fir-hilt/72d5) and e6d9. Genuinely remaining: WS-DOC, WS-EVAL-EXISTING, WS-VISUAL-EVAL.

DECISIONS:
1. WS-EVAL-EXISTING = FULL build (Phase 1 + Phase 2). Phase 1: author the 3 missing eval specs (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) + offline discipline unit tests. Phase 2: make evals actually RUN — the live tier is currently an unwired stub (run_eval() raises NotImplementedError; deterministic scorer names are inert strings with NO registry/dispatch; eval-live promptfoo step is a placeholder echo). Phase 2 sub-deliverables: 2.1 deterministic-scorer registry + ~12 impls; 2.2 live solver w/ context-injection + repo-tool stubbing (the 3 reviewers are store+repo-coupled agentic ops, not inline-feedable); 2.3 per-reviewer dataset schema + validation (incl scan_spec BATCH unit); 2.4 fixture corpus + label adjudication (completion-verifier needs source-citation fixtures); 2.5 Inspect Task glue + eval-live CI wiring (workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule, non-blocking, cached, concurrency-capped, dashboard spend cap). Design aligns with 2025-26 community consensus (two-tier: deterministic gate every PR; LLM-judge off-PR-path, non-blocking). Benefits ALL 7 specs.
2. WS-VISUAL-EVAL = SPLIT OFF into its own epic (out of scope for 6f2d). The epic frames it as a decision WS; carving it out so 6f2d can close on DOC+EVAL.

Eval plan was adversarially reviewed by subagent (community-standards/maintainability/reliability/correctness/fitness); the inert-scorer-registry + agentic-reviewer-not-inline-feedable findings are folded into Phase 2 above.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 PLAN REVIEW — [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: PASS (no findings).

I assessed the epic's CHOSEN APPROACH against all four G6 dimensions, treating the compiled state (body + the authoritative re-triage/scope comments) as one plan, and grounding mechanism reasoning in the live engine code (src/rebar/llm/workflow/*).

(1) MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — sound. This epic's load-bearing mechanism is a PROCESS (capture scope → dedicated brainstorm does the deep design BEFORE implementation → decompose into workstreams), and the mechanism demonstrably executed correctly: the brainstorm produced a grounded architecture decision (comment, session log ad3f), the engine BUILD was correctly spun out into its own epic (e6d9) with this epic set depends_on it, and the visual/validate/migrate workstreams were re-triaged against shipped code. The one open mechanism question the body raises — WS-MIGRATE's CAVEAT that "the engine is a thin LINEAR executor (graphlib) … the gate's dynamic fan-out … may exceed the DSL; resolve whether the DSL gains map/fan-out steps" — is explicitly deferred to the brainstorm AND is already resolved in the shipped code: src/rebar/llm/workflow/map_fanout.py ships bounded-concurrent map fan-out (the SOLE, recorded Burr-tripwire relaxation; order-independent, iteration-keyed idempotency markers, lock-serialized commits), so the fan-out concern does not break the migration approach. The RE-TRIAGE comments correctly reconcile the now-stale seed body against this (e6d9 closed; gates/plan-review.yaml + gates/completion-verification.yaml exist), so the plan's own text resolves the apparent staleness rather than leaving a live mechanism defect. No hidden ordering/atomicity/TOCTOU hazard found.

(2) FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — solves the NAMED problem (a remediation epic capturing the four real gaps: no usage doc, fragmented LLM substrate, silently-deferred bidirectional visual editing, no production-prompt home) rather than a proxy. The narrowed remaining scope (WS-DOC docs/workflow-engine.md; WS-EVAL-EXISTING eval coverage for the migrated reviewers) is the genuine residual, and the latest scope comment grounds it concretely (the WS-EVAL Phase-2 "inert deterministic-scorer registry / agentic-reviewers-not-inline-feedable" findings are real code facts, correctly folded in).

(3) APPROACH SELECTION — WS-VALIDATE is itself a built-in alternatives evaluation, and the architecture-decision comment documents a thorough structural survey (DBOS/Restate/SpiffWorkflow-BPMN/Temporal/Prefect/Dagster/LangGraph/n8n/Node-RED/Argo) with the engine kept-and-rebuilt on a defensible constraint (event-sourced run-state on a git-backed event log that no off-the-shelf durable-execution library can defer to). I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) adopt an off-the-shelf engine (SpiffWorkflow/Temporal) instead of the homegrown interpreter, and (b) keep LLM logic bespoke and skip the engine-unification — and both are defensibly INFERIOR on the named constraints (off-the-shelf engines own their persistence and can't sit on the git event log; bespoke duplication is the exact fragmentation the epic exists to remove). No clearly-superior missed alternative; alternatives discarded, not written into the plan (anti-priming respected). The "don't reinvent — adopt an established visual-editing ecosystem" concern further shows the anti-NIH discipline applied to itself (resolved as ephemeral bpmn-js projection in e6d9).

(4) POSITIVE RATIONALE — present and abundant: build-not-adopt is justified by the event-sourced/git-text axle; the provider-agnostic runtime is justified by the cross-provider hard requirement + Langfuse going OTel-native; visual editing as an ephemeral non-persisted projection is justified by the n8n/Windmill dual-store reconciliation cautionary tale. No rejected-alternatives section is forced into the implementer-facing plan (correct anti-priming).

ANTI-FP honored: this is a genuine design/scoping epic with real structural choices (engine build vs adopt; visual format; substrate unification), so alternative-generation was warranted; correctness reasoning was grounded in the actual engine code (map_fanout.py, gates/*.yaml, the depends_on→e6d9 split), not manufactured.

affirmations: G6 — mechanism correctness (process mechanism executed correctly; the one open DSL-fanout question is both brainstorm-deferred and already resolved in shipped map_fanout.py); fitness-for-purpose (captures the four real gaps; residual scope grounded in code); approach selection defensible vs reviewer-generated alternatives (engine build-not-adopt + ephemeral visual projection, no clearly-superior missed option); positive rationale stated for every major chosen approach.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: CLEAN — no findings.

T8 APPLIES: this epic's plan text defines/references LLM-review-system constructs (the criteria registry with `exec` tiers, the ISF "intent-source fidelity" criterion + its 2-step protocol, the agent-vs-single-turn bright line, the three-pass evidence/verify/gate model, and the Pass-4 MOVE REGISTRY referenced in the seed concerns). I tool-grounded each referenced enum/schema/protocol/artifact and probed for the five structural-gap classes T8 hunts. No proven gap; the constructs this epic names are fully specified in already-shipped sibling machinery (epics 5fd2/9da1/e6d9, all closed) and resolve to real source.

Structural-gap checklist (all PASS):
- (a) No referenced enum whose value vocabulary is undefined. The criterion exec-tier vocabulary `DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT` is fully enumerated and closed in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:31` (merged-descriptor `"exec"`) and used consistently in the plan's bright-line prose. The ISF verdict/three-pass `{yes|no|insufficient}` + severity-attribute vocab are defined in 5fd2/9da1 and the shipped passes, not left open here.
- (b) The ISF processing protocol/decision rule is co-located with the schema/context it needs: the plan states ISF fires ONLY when the ticket is LINKED TO A SESSION LOG, runs on a frontier model, and is a 2-STEP "extract expressed requirements → check the plan-graph against each" comparison over artifacts already held (session log + pre-resolved ticket graph) — firing condition, tier, and protocol are stated together. The criterion itself is shipped + validated under closed story 681b (registry.py ISF, prompt files plan_review_ISF.md/isf_finder.md/isf_summarizer.md, eval `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml`).
- (c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. This is a remediation/doc/eval epic (WS-DOC, WS-EVAL-EXISTING, WS-VISUAL-EVAL remaining); it introduces no new LLM-decision counter/state of its own — the finding-`id` mint + advisory cap remain owned by the orchestrator (ca03) in 5fd2.
- (d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified. The ISF criterion specifies the context-overflow fallback explicitly (a DETERMINISTIC pre-retrieval step — chunk/grep decision-bearing sections + pre-fetch the ticket graph — "never an autonomous agent loop"); the no-linked-intent-source case is a specified not-applicable/skip ("never fabricate an intent baseline"); the gate's fail-open/INDETERMINATE postures are inherited from the shipped three-pass design.
- (e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern. The epic AFFIRMATIVELY documents this very concern — the "Anti-false-positive framing must be PINK-ELEPHANT-SAFE (affirmative)" bullet and the prompt-engineering-guidance bullet prescribe affirmative framing over negation and atomic checklists co-located with each criterion — so the antipattern is handled by design, not present. Referenced agents/skills/enums all exist (tool-verified): the criteria registry + exec-tier vocab, the ISF eval spec, and the three-pass passes resolve to real source.

Affirmations (T8): every referenced LLM-system enum/protocol/schema/artifact this epic names exists and is fully specified (tool-verified); the ISF decision rule is co-located with its firing condition + 2-step protocol + overflow fallback; no undefined-but-referenced vocabulary, no ambiguous counter, no unspecified sub-step fallback, and no instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern (the epic prescribes the affirmative substitute). No T8 findings to action.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Grounded the correctness reasoning in the live eval seam (src/rebar/llm/eval.py) and the existing packaged specs (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/), plus both children (rink-cloud-gum/b4af, seek-ditch-corgi/4024).

MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (PASS):
- Two-tier eval model (deterministic gate every PR at zero API cost + LLM-judge tier off the PR critical path) is directly enforced by the existing discipline: validate_eval_spec requires ≥1 DETERMINISTIC gating scorer (eval.py:219-220) and llm-judge scorers must carry gates:false (validate_scorer eval.py:124-127, "judges REPORT"). The offline discipline (validate_eval_spec/at_least_passes/coverage/cohens_kappa/to_junit) is genuinely stdlib-only/offline-testable (eval.py:19-21), so the "no model call on every PR" tier is achievable as written.
- Scorer registry (AC "every scorer name maps to an implemented pure function; validate_scorer rejects an unregistered name") correctly targets a real gap: today validate_scorer only checks a deterministic scorer HAS a name (eval.py:120-123) — names are inert. The proposed registry + rejection is the right closure of that hole.
- run_eval() wiring (AC "no longer raises NotImplementedError") is the seam's stated intended completion: the Inspect Task is assembled from the already-validated spec (eval.py:341-345, currently NotImplementedError at 346-351). Sound.
- Context-injection solver for the 3 store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers (AC5) is structurally correct: these reviewers read the store+repo via tools at runtime, so a static inline input would be unfaithful; context-injection + stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools preserves production fidelity and remains deterministic for CI.
- eval-live rails (workflow_dispatch + weekly, continue-on-error, response-cached, concurrency-capped, no live call on fork PRs/Dependabot) are standard, correct hardening for a credentialed live-LLM job.

FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE (PASS): the success criterion ("a prompt edit is regression-gated by an eval that actually SCORES the output, not one that only shape-validates the spec") solves the named problem rather than a proxy — it directly addresses the current "validated for shape but never scored" state.

APPROACH SELECTION (PASS): generated two structurally-different alternatives and discarded both as inferior — (a) single-tier "gate everything including the LLM judge on every PR" is rejected by the codebase's own hard rule that judge non-determinism must not create false confidence; (b) inline-feeding the agentic reviewers a captured transcript instead of a context-injection solver is less faithful to production tool-reading behavior. The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, testability, simplicity, and robustness. (Alternatives intentionally NOT written into the plan.)

POSITIVE RATIONALE (PASS, borderline): the why-this-architecture rationale lives mainly in child b4af ("regression-gated... not a blind cutover"; "cheap deterministic gate every PR + expensive LLM-judge tier OFF the PR critical path") rather than in the epic body's own prose, which is thinner here. Per pass-1 decisiveness guidance, the plan + its linked child affirmatively carry the rationale, so this PASSES rather than producing a manufactured MINOR finding. (Not emitted as a finding.)

AFFIRMATIONS: mechanism achieves the goal; solves the named problem not a proxy; chosen approach defensible vs generated alternatives; positive rationale present (via the child).

Empty findings list for G6.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T1] Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (overlay) — VERDICT: 1 finding (the rest of the bright-line surface PASSES with prior art well-handled).

Bright lines that fire here: external integration (Inspect AI eval harness; CI with live LLM provider calls), a migration ("unify LLM functionality on the engine"), and a novel pattern (context-injection solver + stubbed repo tools for the agentic reviewers; two-tier eval model; three-pass review). Most of these are PASSES — see affirmations — because the patterns are already established in-repo (src/rebar/llm/eval.py) and the plan/child build on them rather than reinventing. The single open T1 item is the one genuinely new mechanism that no prior art covers and whose enabling Inspect capability is asserted but unresolved.

FINDINGS (T1):

- [ ] Resolve the unverified Inspect-AI capability assertion underlying AC5's "context-injection solver plus stubbed or fixture-backed repo tools" before committing the design. (criteria: T1) — AC5 requires the three store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers to run under Inspect via a solver that injects repo/store context and stubs or fixture-backs the repo TOOLS. This mechanism does not exist anywhere in the codebase yet (search for `solver`/`context-injection` under src/rebar/llm returns only the comment placeholder at eval.py:342; eval.py:341-346 explicitly leaves the live Task wiring `dataset → solver(prompt) → scorers → epochs` unbuilt and `run_eval()` raising NotImplementedError). T1(c) asks that "library supports X" assertions be resolved: the load-bearing assertion is that Inspect's solver/tool API actually supports injecting per-sample context AND swapping the reviewer's repo-tool implementations for stubs/fixtures at eval time. Pin this to a concrete Inspect API (e.g. which solver primitive and which tool-override seam, at the INSPECT_MIN_VERSION 0.3.221 floor in eval.py:40) so the design isn't committed on an unverified capability.
  location: AC line 5 ("`run_eval()` runs a real Inspect eval … context-injection solver plus stubbed or fixture-backed repo tools"); child rink-cloud-gum Phase-2.2/2.5; src/rebar/llm/eval.py:40,319-351
  evidence: [
    "AC5: 'the three store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers run via a context-injection solver plus stubbed or fixture-backed repo tools'",
    "ABSENCE: no solver/context-injection implementation exists (search_files `solver`/`context-injection` under src/rebar/llm yields only eval.py:342 placeholder comment); run_eval() still raises NotImplementedError (eval.py:346)",
    "eval.py:40 pins INSPECT_MIN_VERSION = '0.3.221' as the API floor, but the solver/tool-override capability that AC5 depends on is named, not verified against that API",
    "child rink-cloud-gum 'Why': 'the 3 reviewers read the store + repo via tools, so they are NOT inline-feedable' — i.e. the design hinges on tool-stubbing being achievable in Inspect"
  ]
  suggested_fix: In AC5 (or the child's Phase-2.2 task), name the concrete Inspect AI primitive(s) the solver uses to (1) inject per-sample repo/store context into the reviewer transcript and (2) substitute stub/fixture repo-tool implementations for the live ones, and confirm those exist at the 0.3.221 floor. If the tool-override seam is not first-class in Inspect, capture the fallback (e.g. wrapping the reviewer behind a rebar-side tool registry that the eval swaps) so the design is not committed on an unconfirmed library capability.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 criteria already satisfied — emitted as positive feedback, not findings):
- Prior art for the eval seam is CONSIDERED, not reinvented: the plan extends the existing Inspect-AI machinery in src/rebar/llm/eval.py (grader discipline, Cohen's-kappa judge alignment, at_least(k) gate with banned pass@k, promptfoo/JUnit interop, the 4 packaged specs) to 7 specs rather than building a parallel harness — AC3/AC4 and child rink-cloud-gum's "all 7 specs" reconcile to 4 existing + 3 new.
- The two-tier eval model (zero-cost deterministic gate every PR + non-blocking, cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe live tier on dispatch/weekly schedule) is a well-trodden CI pattern, not a novel architecture — no prior-art search owed (anti-FP); the child explicitly notes alignment with community consensus.
- The migration's prior art is cited rather than re-litigated: WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN are recorded as DONE via closed sibling epics (5766/72d5/e6d9), and the earlier open-ended decisions (DSL map/fan-out, cleanup set, visual-format choice) are stated as already settled there — no reinvention in this epic.
- The grader-discipline rules (cross-family judge, gates:false, pinned dated snapshot, coverage threshold, non-empty gold_set) in AC3 mirror the established validate_scorer/validate_eval_spec contract already in eval.py — repackaging of an in-repo standard, correctly reused.
- The three-pass review pattern and llm-framework seam referenced for docs (AC2) are existing, documented artifacts (docs/llm-framework.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md) the plan cross-links rather than re-derives.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T8] LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe (overlay) — VERDICT: 2 findings (both grounded against the live eval seam).

Overlay attaches: the epic defines an LLM/eval system (packaged eval specs, deterministic-scorer registry, a context-injection solver running 3 agentic reviewers, llm-judge graders). Probed the five structural sub-checks tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/eval.py and the shipped specs under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/.

FINDING 1 (T8 · undefined-but-referenced value vocabulary):

- [ ] The new datasets' label/`expect` value vocabulary is referenced (AC6 "dataset balance"; AC3/AC6 "non-empty gold_set") but never defined for the 3 NEW reviewers, whose output shape differs from the existing specs whose vocabulary is hardcoded in the offline tests.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: parent AC line "Eval specs exist for completion-verifier, ticket-quality, and spec-alignment ... a non-empty gold_set" and AC line "offline unit tests assert dataset balance and a non-empty gold_set"; child rink-cloud-gum (b4af) Phase-2.3 "per-reviewer dataset schema + validation"; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:49-104; tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41.
  - evidence:
    - The shipped specs encode a reviewer-specific label/expect enum: `dataset[].expect` ∈ {high_validity, low_validity, finding, pass} and `gold_set[].label` ∈ {should_drop, should_uphold} (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:54/63/96-104).
    - The offline "balance" assertion is hardcoded to THAT vocabulary: test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41 buckets cases by `expect in ("finding","high_validity")` vs `("pass","low_validity")`. The 3 new reviewers have different output shapes (completion-verifier → PASS/FAIL with source-citation, per b4af Phase-2.4; ticket-quality → a quality grade; spec-alignment → alignment verdict), so they need their OWN expect/label vocabulary.
    - ABSENCE: neither the parent ACs nor b4af defines what label/expect values the new datasets use, nor how the new "dataset balance" assertion recognizes them. `validate_eval_spec` (eval.py:191-223) does not check the dataset/gold_set label vocabulary at all (it validates epochs/gate/coverage/scorers only), so the executor has no schema to follow — the vocabulary is load-bearing for the AC6 balance gate yet unspecified.
  - checklist_item: No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined.
  - suggested_fix: In b4af Phase-2.3 (per-reviewer dataset schema), pin the `expect`/`label` value vocabulary for each of the 3 reviewers (e.g. completion-verifier: expect ∈ {should_fail, should_pass} with required source-citation field on FAIL fixtures) and state how the offline dataset-balance assertion buckets those values, so AC6's "dataset balance" check has a defined per-reviewer vocabulary rather than reusing the verifier's {high_validity/low_validity} bins.

FINDING 2 (T8 · unspecified fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step):

- [ ] The context-injection solver's fallback for a fixture-MISS in the stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools is unspecified, yet a miss directly affects whether the coverage_threshold gate is met.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: parent AC line "the three store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers run via a context-injection solver plus stubbed or fixture-backed repo tools"; child b4af Phase-2.2 "live solver w/ context-injection + repo-tool stubbing"; AC line "coverage threshold"; src/rebar/llm/eval.py:234-240 (coverage / coverage_ok), eval.py:319-351 (the run_eval seam to be wired).
  - evidence:
    - The 3 reviewers read the store + repo via TOOLS, not inline (confirmed agentic per the G6 comment grounding verify_completion → gate_dispatch reading store+repo, completion.py:169-225), so the solver must service tool calls from a stub/fixture corpus.
    - `coverage_threshold` is a hard gate: coverage() = scored/total and coverage_ok() compares it to the threshold (eval.py:234-240); a sample that can't be scored lowers coverage.
    - ABSENCE: the plan does not specify what a fixture-backed repo tool returns when the reviewer requests a path/ticket NOT in the fixture corpus — error (abort the sample), empty result (reviewer proceeds on partial context, risking a non-deterministic/unscored sample), or skip (sample drops out of `total`). Each choice moves the coverage number differently, so leaving it unspecified makes the AC's coverage_threshold gate behavior ambiguous for the new agentic reviewers.
  - checklist_item: A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified.
  - suggested_fix: In b4af Phase-2.2, specify the fixture-miss contract for the repo-tool stubs — e.g. a fixture miss raises a deterministic "out-of-fixture" error that marks the sample unscored (so it counts against coverage and is visible), rather than silently returning empty context; document this so the coverage_threshold gate has well-defined semantics under partial fixtures.

Affirmations (T8 sub-checks that PASS):
- Counter/state increment placement — CLEAN. The at_least(k)/epoch pass-tally (at_least_passes, eval.py:229-231) and coverage tally (eval.py:234-236) have unambiguous, co-located definitions; no ambiguously-placed increment is introduced.
- Processing protocol co-located with its schema — CLEAN. The grader-discipline decision rules (cross-family, temperature 0, integer seed, dated snapshot, gates:false) are co-located with the scorer schema they validate in validate_scorer (eval.py:107-166), and the parent AC restates them at the spec level.
- Referenced registry/seam exist and are specified as deliverables, not dangling references — CLEAN/PASS. The "deterministic-scorer registry … validate_scorer rejects an unregistered name" (AC4) and the run_eval() live-harness replacement (AC5) are explicit BUILD deliverables; the current stubs (validate_scorer only name-checks deterministic scorers eval.py:120-123; run_eval raises NotImplementedError eval.py:346-351) are correctly identified as the work, so this is a specified gap-to-fill, not a T8 under-specification.
- Pink-elephant / instruction-locality — CLEAN. No antipattern in the plan's prompt/criteria handling surfaced.

Two PROVEN structural gaps emitted (new-reviewer label vocabulary; fixture-miss fallback); the remaining T8 sub-checks pass.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G3] Child coverage — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child 4024 WS-DOC) — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Authoritative residual roster = 2 children: WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af). 4-bucket audit of every parent acceptance/success criterion, with absence cross-checked against the whole roster:

ACCEPTANCE:
1. docs/workflow-engine.md exists, linked from README + CLAUDE.md index, CLIENT-vs-REBAR-DEV split — FULLY (this child, WS-DOC AC1+AC2).
2. Documents YAML DSL, scripted-vs-agentic, agent-vs-single-turn bright line, three-pass pattern, eval seam (two-tier/live-trigger/cost+fork-safety rails), cross-links llm-framework.md + plan-review-gate.md not-duplicate — FULLY (WS-DOC AC3 covers DSL/scripted-agentic/bright-line/three-pass; AC4 covers eval-seam two-tier+live-trigger+cost/fork-safety; AC5 covers cross-link-don't-duplicate / no-contradiction).
3. Eval specs for completion-verifier/ticket-quality/spec-alignment, validate_eval_spec-clean, cross-family judge (gates:false), at_least(k)/epochs, coverage threshold, non-empty gold_set — FULLY (sibling b4af AC1).
4. Deterministic-scorer registry; every scorer name maps to pure fn; validate_scorer rejects unregistered — FULLY (sibling b4af AC2).
5. run_eval() runs real Inspect eval (no NotImplementedError); 3 agentic reviewers via context-injection solver + stubbed/fixture repo tools — FULLY (sibling b4af AC3).
6. eval-discipline CI validates all packaged specs every PR, no model call; offline tests assert dataset balance + non-empty gold_set — FULLY (sibling b4af AC4).
7. eval-live CI on workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule, non-blocking, response-cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe; spend-cap guidance documented — FULLY (sibling b4af AC5).
8. Every child ticket closed (each signed by completion-verifier gate) — STRUCTURAL/process (covered-by-definition by the gate-closure process over the 2-child roster; not a content deliverable, not an uncovered finding).

SUCCESS:
S1. New contributor can read the doc and decide workflow-vs-bespoke-op + how to run/trigger evals, without reading source — FULLY (this child: WS-DOC scope "when to use a workflow vs a bespoke op" + AC2 client run/use guidance + AC4 how live tier is triggered).
S2. Prompt edit to any packaged reviewer prompt is regression-gated by an eval that actually SCORES output, not shape-validates — FULLY (sibling b4af — its stated purpose: "regression-gated, not a blind cutover"; run_eval() actually scores rather than shape-validating).

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered. The criteria this child (WS-DOC) owns are fully covered by its own ACs; the eval-execution criteria are fully covered by sibling WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af) in the roster; the child-closure criterion is structural. Empty findings list for G3 on this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Both remaining workstreams delivered and their stories closed+signed: WS-EVAL-EXISTING (rink-cloud-gum) and WS-DOC (seek-ditch-corgi). WS-VISUAL-EVAL split to epic yip-gem-teak (out of scope). Engine/migration/cleanup/visual/prompts/intent-align were already done via closed sibling epics A/B/e6d9. Code shipped on branch feat/ws-eval-existing-6f2d (PR #62), rebased onto current main, full mypy/ruff/tests green, live path smoke-validated. Closing the epic.

compiled_state.description:
# Workflow-engine remediation: unify LLM functionality on the engine, restore visual editing, document intended use

## Context

Captures the remediation-on-top-of-the-engine scope that surfaced finalizing the
plan-review gate. The original long-form scope-capture prose (workstream catalog +
research notes) is preserved in this ticket's EDIT HISTORY and in the re-triage
COMMENTS below; this body is the current, executable state. Related: engine epic
`hump-seam-spice` (e6d9, closed), gate epic `messy-moose-jig` (5fd2, closed),
three-pass epic `sheer-myth-idol` (9da1).

## Current scope & disposition (2026-06-27)

The historical workstreams are RESOLVED; this is the authoritative current state:

- DONE via closed sibling epics — WS-INTENT-ALIGN, WS-MIGRATE, WS-PROMPTS, WS-CLEANUP,
  WS-VISUAL, WS-VALIDATE: delivered by epic A `sage-votary-logic` (5766), epic B
  `peak-fir-hilt` (72d5), and engine epic e6d9. The earlier open-ended decision
  investigations (DSL map/fan-out, exact cleanup set, visual-format choice) were
  settled there — they are no longer open questions in this epic.
- SPLIT OUT — WS-VISUAL-EVAL is moved to its own epic `yip-gem-teak` (7013); OUT OF
  SCOPE here.
- REMAINING (this epic's only live deliverables) — WS-DOC and WS-EVAL-EXISTING,
  decomposed into child stories `seek-ditch-corgi` (WS-DOC) and `rink-cloud-gum`
  (WS-EVAL-EXISTING; task children: scorer registry + dataset schema, the 3 specs,
  the live solver + fixtures, and the run_eval/CI wiring).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` exists, is linked from README and the CLAUDE.md doc index, and separates a CLIENT (run/use) audience from a REBAR-DEV (author/edit) audience.
- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` documents the YAML DSL, scripted-vs-agentic steps, the agent-vs-single-turn bright line, the three-pass review pattern, and the eval seam (two-tier model, how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails); it cross-links rather than duplicates docs/llm-framework.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md.
- [ ] Eval specs exist for `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, and `spec-alignment` under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, each passing `validate_eval_spec` with a cross-family judge (gates:false), `at_least(k)` over explicit epochs, a coverage threshold, and a non-empty gold_set.
- [ ] A deterministic-scorer registry exists; every scorer name used by the packaged specs maps to an implemented pure function, and `validate_scorer` rejects an unregistered name.
- [ ] `run_eval()` runs a real live eval (no longer raises NotImplementedError) via a native loop over the eval solver (Inspect AI intentionally not used — agentic ops don't fit its single-completion model; rationale in eval.py); the three store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers run against a disposable fixture store via the solver.
- [ ] `eval-discipline` CI validates all packaged specs on every PR with no model call; offline unit tests assert dataset balance and a non-empty gold_set.
- [ ] `eval-live` CI runs on workflow_dispatch plus a weekly schedule, is non-blocking (continue-on-error), response-cached, concurrency-capped, and fork-safe (no live call on fork PRs or Dependabot); provider spend-cap guidance is documented.
- [ ] Every child ticket of this epic is closed (each signed by the completion-verifier gate).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A new contributor can read `docs/workflow-engine.md` and correctly decide when to author a workflow vs a bespoke op, and how to run and trigger evals, without reading source.
- [ ] A prompt edit to any packaged reviewer prompt is regression-gated by an eval that actually scores the output, not one that only shape-validates the spec.
compiled_state.title: Workflow-engine remediation: unify LLM functionality on the engine, restore visual editing, document intended use
